description: Client library published to PyPI that evaluates proposed agent tool actions against the Sector8 Gate (POST /api/v1/evaluate), optionally sends telemetry, and ships sector8-verify onboarding and sector8-guard MCP installer CLIs. Scope is the SDK package in sector8-sdk-python, not the Gate service itself. Assumptions: customers run the SDK inside their own application process; production uses https://sdkapi.sector8.ai with HTTPS host allowlisting unless unsafe_endpoint is explicitly set; runtime keys are one-time-reveal secrets held in environment or app config.

assets.name: Proposed tool arguments and LLM prompt/completion telemetry
Decision artifact integrity (ALLOW/DENY outcome and trace fields)
Host application actions gated before execution

trust_boundaries.name: SDK HTTP client to Sector8 Gate API
Developer workstation to project MCP config
PyPI registry to customer install environment

entry_points.name: Application calls client.evaluate() and branches on decision.allowed
SDK POST /api/v1/evaluate with X-API-Key and X-Client-Id headers
SDK POST telemetry, threat-alerts, or incident-logs endpoints
sector8-verify reads SECTOR8_* environment and calls evaluate fixtures
sector8-guard install writes sector8-guard MCP entry to .claude/settings.json
pip install sector8-sdk from PyPI into customer environment

threats.title: Host application executes privileged tool after DENY or SDK error instead of failing closed
Host application bypasses evaluate() on sensitive code paths while still importing the SDK
Misconfigured unsafe_endpoint sends runtime API key to attacker-controlled origin
Network adversary tampers with evaluate response so host app treats malformed payload as ALLOW
Telemetry upload exfiltrates customer prompt and completion content containing secrets
Onboarding verifier run in shared CI exposes runtime key via environment dump or verbose logs
sector8-guard install repoints MCP stdio server to attacker script that reads SECTOR8_API_KEY from env
Typosquat PyPI package substitutes malicious client that exfiltrates keys on sector8.setup()

mitigations.description: README and SDK contract require executing actions only when decision.allowed is True; authentication and validation failures raise errors and must not be treated as ALLOW
validate_base_url restricts remote traffic to sdkapi.sector8.ai by default; arbitrary hosts require explicit unsafe_endpoint
Plain HTTP rejected unless unsafe_endpoint is set; production docs omit staging override variables
DecisionArtifact.from_payload validates required fields and rejects inconsistent ALLOW/DENY payloads
Customers control telemetry payload content; SDK sends only fields the application passes; docs warn against logging secrets in prompts
sector8-verify prints decision trace IDs only and does not echo API keys or request payloads on success
sector8-guard install prompts before overwriting existing MCP entry and writes repo-relative command paths
Official PyPI package name is sector8-sdk; README instructs customers to pin approved versions and avoid look-alike package names
